title: Cholesterol makes cells’ nuclei squishy, helping melanoma spread
description: When cholesterol levels in the nuclear envelope were high, the nucleus became easier to deform. This is important because cancer cells often have to squeeze through tight gaps between other cells to spread. A squishier nucleus makes that squeezing easier, so the cancer can invade new tissues more successfully
